findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion (with a `pytest -k` proving command) that verifies a project code-review criterion with no eval spec raises a located error naming the criterion.
evidence: 'Edge: a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error.' — Testing section
No AC references this edge case; both ACs only cover the happy path and parity path.
findings.finding: The edge-case behavior 'a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error' is described in the Testing section but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion with a proving command, leaving it as an unchecked completion claim.
findings.location: Testing section (edge case bullet) / Acceptance Criteria section (no matching AC)
scenarios: An executor ships the happy path and parity tests, marks the ticket done, and the error-path behavior is never verified or may be missing entirely.
The error may be unlocated (not naming the criterion) and no AC would catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] A project code-review criterion whose `.eval.md` is absent raises a `ValueError` (or equivalent) whose message names the criterion id; verified by `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k "eval and no_spec"` asserting the exception message contains the criterion id.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion covering the edge case: a project code-review criterion with no `.eval.md` spec raises a located error naming the criterion, verified by the `-k eval` test suite.
evidence: Approach: 'A project criterion with no eval spec raises a **located** error naming the criterion.'
Testing: 'Edge: a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error.'
Acceptance Criteria: only AC#1 (happy path) and AC#2 (parity) are listed; no AC covers the error-raising behavior.
ABSENCE: no `-k eval` selector or other verifier is specified for the edge case.
findings.finding: The edge-case deliverable — 'a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error' — is specified in both the Approach and Testing sections but has no covering Acceptance Criterion, leaving it unverified by the test plan.
findings.location: Approach (last bullet); Testing (Edge case bullet); Acceptance Criteria section
scenarios: An implementer could omit the located-error raise entirely and the two ACs would still pass, shipping a silent failure mode instead of a diagnostic error.
During code review, there is no acceptance gate to verify the error message is actually 'located' (names the criterion).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a third AC such as: '- [ ] A project code-review criterion whose prompt has no eval spec raises a `RebarError` (or equivalent) whose message names the criterion; verified by `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k eval_no_spec`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion for the edge case: a project code-review id with no `.eval.md` spec raises a located error naming the criterion (e.g. verified by the same `-k eval` selector with a `pytest.raises` assertion).
evidence: 'A project criterion with no eval spec raises a **located** error naming the criterion.' — Approach section
Testing section lists it as an edge case but no AC checkbox exists for it
The two ACs only cover the happy path and parity, leaving the error path without a verifiable acceptance gate
findings.finding: The edge-case behaviour — 'a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error' — is described in both Approach and Testing, but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion checkbox, so it lacks an end-state-observable proof at the AC level.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (missing edge-case AC); Testing section ('Edge: a project code-review id with no eval spec raises a located error')
scenarios: If the error path regresses (e.g. raises an unlocated or wrong exception), no AC would catch it; a reviewer could mark the plan done without validating this contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a third AC checkbox: '- [ ] A project code-review criterion whose eval spec is absent raises a located error that names the criterion; verified by `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k eval_missing_spec`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `_code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id)` call site at `eval_solver.py:279` in the Scope section as also needing update to `_code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id, repo_root)` when the function signature gains `repo_root`.
evidence: Current code at eval_solver.py:279: `if _code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id) is not None:`
Plan Approach: 'thread `repo_root` (already available and passed to `_run_code_review_case` at `eval_solver.py:204-234`)' — implies signature change but doesn't name the call-site update.
Scope section lists `eval_solver.py — _code_review_prompt_id repo_root/gate-aware admission` but does not enumerate the call site at line 279 as needing update.
findings.finding: The `_code_review_prompt_id` call site at `eval_solver.py:279` uses only one argument (`_code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id)`), but the plan adds a `repo_root` parameter to the function. That call site must also be updated to pass `repo_root`, yet the Scope section only mentions changes to the function definition — the call-site update is unenumerated.
scenarios: If the call site at line 279 is not updated alongside the function signature change, a TypeError occurs at runtime for any code-review eval run.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet for eval_solver.py: 'also update the call site at line 279 to pass `repo_root`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the id-translation step in the `_code_review_prompt_id` extension: either (a) derive the logical id from the physical prompt id before consulting `effective_criteria()` (e.g. strip `code-review-` prefix, recover `project.foo` from `project-foo`), or (b) have `_code_review_prompt_id` build the expected physical ids from `effective_criteria()` using the gate-specific forward map and check membership in that derived set.
evidence: `effective_criteria()` at `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py:179` delegates to `_criteria.effective_criteria(repo_root, gate_key=_GATE_KEY)` which returns logical criterion ids from `criteria/overlay.py:276`
`_code_review_prompt_id` at `eval_solver.py:187` receives a `prompt_id` string (physical), e.g. `code-review-project-foo`, not the logical `project.foo`
The plan says 'consult registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)' but does not specify the id-space translation needed to compare these two vocabularies
findings.finding: `_code_review_prompt_id` is extended to check `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`, but `effective_criteria()` returns LOGICAL criterion ids (e.g. `project.foo`), while `_code_review_prompt_id` receives PHYSICAL prompt ids (e.g. `code-review-project-foo`). The plan does not specify how the physical id is matched against the logical-id vocabulary, leaving an implicit vocabulary mismatch that would cause the guard to always return `None` for project criteria.
findings.location: Approach section — `eval_solver.py` bullet: 'consult `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` … in addition to the static built-in set'
scenarios: `_code_review_prompt_id('code-review-project-foo', repo_root)` checks whether `'code-review-project-foo'` is in `effective_criteria()` which returns `('project.foo', ...)` — the membership test fails and `None` is returned, so the code-review arm is never dispatched.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how fire detection works for code-review verifier cases in `calibrate_criterion` — either define a code-review-aware `_fired` variant keyed on the prompt's output schema type (`findings` vs `verifications`), or explicitly exclude verifier prompts from per-criterion calibration scope.
evidence: Plan: 'the returned output is scored on findings presence (recall / no-fire) or verification presence'
Current `_fired` at eval.py:379-381: `return bool((verdict or {}).get('findings'))` — hardcoded to `'findings'` key.
`_run_code_review_case` docstring: 'a base/overlay reviewer's `{findings:[...]}` ... or the verifier's `{verifications:[...]}`'
The plan specifies no override or variant of `_fired` for the verifier schema, nor does it say verifier cases are excluded from code-review calibration.
findings.finding: `_fired` in `calibrate_criterion` checks `verdict.get('findings')`, but a code-review verifier case (`code-review-verify`) returns `{verifications:[...]}`, not `{findings:[...]}`. The plan states scoring is 'findings presence (recall / no-fire) or verification presence' but never specifies which `_fired` variant applies in the calibration loop for a verifier-type eval spec, leaving the fire-detection protocol for verifier cases undefined.
findings.location: eval.py:379-381 (`_fired`); Approach section — '_run_code_review_case' output schema description
scenarios: A calibration eval spec for `code-review-verify` (verifier prompt) would always report 0 fires because `_fired` checks `findings`, not `verifications`, producing silently wrong recall/false-accept metrics.
An implementor may be unaware this is a gap and ship a verifier calibration that appears to work but measures nothing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Approach the gate-membership predicate used by `calibrate_criterion` to route a criterion_id to the code-review branch (e.g., 'check `criterion_id in code_review_registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` before falling back to plan-review mapping').
evidence: Plan: 'add a code-review branch that resolves the physical prompt id via criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review") when the id belongs to the code-review gate'
Existing code at eval.py:416-420 only checks `startswith('plan-review-')` — no gate-membership check exists.

A logical criterion id `project.foo` could belong to either gate; the code-review registry's effective_criteria must be consulted to determine gate membership, but the plan does not name this lookup in the calibrate_criterion branch.
findings.finding: The `calibrate_criterion` Approach says 'add a code-review branch … when the id belongs to the code-review gate' but never specifies the detection predicate. A logical id like `project.foo` does not start with `code-review-`, so the existing `startswith('plan-review-')` branch already handles such ids incorrectly (mapping them to `plan-review-project-foo`). The plan gives no mechanism to distinguish a plan-review `project.foo` from a code-review `project.foo`.
scenarios: A user calls `calibrate_criterion('project.foo', ...)` for a code-review criterion: with no detection predicate the code falls through to `criterion_prompt_id('project.foo')` → `plan-review-project-foo`, which is wrong.
Alternatively the implementer adds a `startswith('code-review-')` check — but logical ids never have that prefix, so the branch is unreachable for the stated use case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact boolean predicate (e.g., `criterion_id in code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`, or a `gate_key` parameter on `calibrate_criterion`) that guards the new code-review branch in `calibrate_criterion`, so the decision rule is unambiguous to an implementor.
evidence: Plan: 'add a code-review branch that resolves the physical prompt id via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` when the id belongs to the code-review gate'
The phrase 'when the id belongs to the code-review gate' is the decision rule, but the rule itself — what predicate decides membership — is absent from the plan.
Current code at eval.py:416-420 tests only `criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-')`, leaving the else branch to call `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` unconditionally with plan-review semantics.
No gating predicate (e.g., a call to `code_review.registry.effective_criteria` or a prefix check) is specified for the new branch.
findings.finding: The gate-membership predicate used by `calibrate_criterion`'s new code-review branch is undefined. The plan says 'when the id belongs to the code-review gate' but never specifies what boolean test on `criterion_id` triggers the code-review branch vs. the existing plan-review branch. The current code uses `criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-')` as its sole discriminator; the code-review branch needs an analogous predicate (e.g., check against `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`, or check for a `code-review-` prefix, or check a gate-key argument) — none of these is named in the plan.
findings.location: Approach section — eval.py `calibrate_criterion` code-review branch; eval.py:416-420
scenarios: An implementor must invent the discriminator: they might check `criterion_id.startswith('code-review-')`, or consult the effective code-review criteria set, or add a `gate_key` parameter to `calibrate_criterion` — each choice has different behavior for ambiguous or mis-typed ids.
A plan-review criterion id that is also a prefix-match for some code-review id (unlikely but not impossible) could be routed to the wrong branch if a prefix heuristic is used.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state whether `calibrate_criterion` passes the physical prompt id (e.g., `code-review-project-foo`) or the logical criterion id (`project.foo`) to `solve(prompt_id, case)`, and confirm that `_code_review_prompt_id` (after extension) will correctly admit whichever form is passed.
evidence: Plan: 'extend `_code_review_prompt_id` to also admit ids present in the code-review effective registry — consult `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`'
Current `_code_review_prompt_id` at eval_solver.py:194: `if not prompt_id.startswith('code-review-'): return None` — logical id `project.foo` is rejected immediately.

`run_case` at eval_solver.py:271-280 checks `_criterion_id` first (plan-review only), then `_code_review_prompt_id`; no code-review logical-id recovery analogous to the `project-` → `project.` rewrite in `_criterion_id` is mentioned.

The plan says `calibrate_criterion` resolves via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` to get the physical id — but it is unclear whether that physical id or the logical id is what gets passed to `solve(prompt_id, case)` and then into `run_case`.
findings.finding: The id form passed through the `run_case` dispatch chain for a project code-review criterion is unspecified. `run_case` checks `_criterion_id` (plan-review, strips `plan-review-` prefix, checks logical ids) then `_code_review_prompt_id` (checks `code-review-` prefix). If a caller passes the logical id `project.foo` for a code-review criterion, it fails both checks: `_criterion_id` won't find it in the plan-review registry, and `_code_review_prompt_id` rejects it because it lacks the `code-review-` prefix. The plan says `_code_review_prompt_id` should 'admit ids present in the code-review effective registry' but does not specify whether callers pass the logical id or the already-translated physical id.
scenarios: If `calibrate_criterion` passes the physical id `code-review-project-foo` to `solve`, then `_code_review_prompt_id` needs to admit it via the effective registry check (it currently only allows static built-ins); this is covered if the registry lookup is added.
If `calibrate_criterion` passes the logical id `project.foo` to `solve`, neither existing arm in `run_case` will dispatch it correctly — a `ValueError` for unknown id would be raised.
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest path: `calibrate_criterion` resolves to the physical id via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` and passes that physical id to `solve`; `_code_review_prompt_id` then extends its allowlist check to include any id returned by `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` (which would yield `project.foo`, so it must also translate via `criterion_prompt_id` before checking). State this translation chain explicitly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the expected path template and minimal required fields for the `.eval.md` fixture (e.g., `<synthetic_repo>/.rebar/evals/code-review-project-foo.eval.md` with a `cases:` key), or reference an existing example.
evidence: 'adding a `.eval.md` spec for `project.foo`' — no path template or format reference given
No pointer to an existing `.eval.md` for a built-in criterion to use as a template
findings.finding: The plan instructs the executor to add a `.eval.md` spec for `project.foo` in the synthetic-repo fixture but does not specify the expected file path or format, leaving the executor to infer the convention.
findings.location: Scope / Testing section — 'reuses child 2's synthetic-repo fixture, adding a `.eval.md` spec for `project.foo`'
scenarios: Executor places the file at an incorrect path or with wrong frontmatter, causing the test to fail silently or the fixture to be invalid (VERIFY-COMMAND-DEFECT type 4: fixture invalidity).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` and `load_eval_spec` I/O failures (missing/malformed registry, invalid repo_root) — at minimum, catch and re-raise with a located message naming the criterion and repo_root, consistent with the 'located error' contract already stated for the no-eval-spec case.
evidence: Plan specifies: 'A project criterion with no eval spec raises a **located** error naming the criterion' — but is silent on failures from `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` itself.
ABSENCE: no retry, graceful-degradation, or catch-and-re-raise with location for `effective_criteria` or `load_eval_spec` I/O failures.
New file I/O failure points introduced in `_code_review_prompt_id` and `calibrate_criterion` code-review branch.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new file-I/O call sites (`registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` and `load_eval_spec(prompt_id, repo_root=repo_root)`) inside the eval resolution path but specifies error handling only for the 'no eval spec' case; failures from a missing/malformed registry file, an invalid `repo_root`, or other I/O errors in `effective_criteria` are unaddressed and will surface as unlocated exceptions to the caller.
scenarios: Caller passes an invalid or missing `repo_root`; `effective_criteria` raises an OSError or parse error; exception propagates without context, making it hard to diagnose which criterion or repo caused the failure.
Registry file is malformed (YAML/TOML parse error); unlocated traceback surfaces instead of a helpful message.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, add: 'If `effective_criteria(repo_root)` raises (I/O or parse error), catch and re-raise as a located `RebarError` naming `repo_root` and the criterion id. This is symmetric with the no-eval-spec located error already specified.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how `criterion_prompt_id` produces a `code-review-project-<name>` prompt id: either extend `ids.py` to accept a `gate_key` / prefix argument (and add it to scope), or use a direct inline mapping (`f"code-review-{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"`) analogous to the existing plan-review logic — and confirm the approach matches the linked cross-reference `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]`.
evidence: Plan quote: 'add a code-review branch that resolves the physical prompt id via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]'
ids.py:36: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str:` — no gate_key parameter
ids.py:31: `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "plan-review-"` — hardcoded plan-review prefix
ids.py:42: `return f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — always emits `plan-review-*`
No existing `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant or gate-keyed variant of `criterion_prompt_id` found in codebase search
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` is cited in the Approach section as the resolver for the code-review calibrate_criterion branch, but the actual function (`ids.py:36`) has signature `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str` with no `gate_key` parameter and unconditionally prepends `"plan-review-"`. For a code-review project criterion `project.foo`, this would return `plan-review-project-foo` — the plan-review physical id — rather than the correct `code-review-project-foo`. The plan's scope omits any extension to `ids.py` or a gate-keyed equivalent.
scenarios: Developer implements the calibrate_criterion branch calling the existing `criterion_prompt_id` as specified; `load_eval_spec` is called with `plan-review-project-foo` instead of `code-review-project-foo`, so the eval spec for the code-review criterion is never found and raises EvalError.
Developer extends `criterion_prompt_id` with a `gate_key` param to fix the mismatch, but that extension is not listed in the Scope section and therefore not tested or reviewed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or specify how `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch maps a code-review criterion id to its physical prompt id — `criterion_prompt_id` has no `gate_key` parameter and always produces `plan-review-*`; the plan must either specify a new function, a new overload, or a direct mapping (e.g. `code-review-project-<name>`) to replace the cited call.
evidence: Plan states: 'resolves the physical prompt id via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")`'
src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str:` — single parameter, no gate_key
ids.py always maps to `plan-review-<id>` prefix, no code-review prefix variant exists
No gate_key parameter found in any search of criteria/ids.py
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` does not accept a `gate_key` parameter — the plan's call site `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` references a non-existent overload; the actual function at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36` takes only `criterion_id` and always returns `plan-review-*`.
scenarios: The `calibrate_criterion` code-review branch as described will fail with a TypeError at call time because `criterion_prompt_id` does not accept `gate_key`.
Even if the call were patched, the returned prompt_id would be `plan-review-project-foo` not `code-review-project-foo`, defeating the purpose of the branch.
findings.suggested_fix: Define the mapping explicitly: for a code-review criterion `project.<name>`, the physical prompt id is `code-review-project-<name>` (mirroring the `plan-review-project-<name>` pattern). The branch can compute this directly as `f"code-review-{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` or via a new `criterion_prompt_id` variant, without inventing a `gate_key` parameter that does not exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope section: `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` is a new file (not an extension of an existing one); note that the synthetic-repo fixture to reuse is in `tests/unit/test_eval_solver_project_criterion.py`.
evidence: Directory listing of tests/unit/ shows no `test_code_review_project_criteria.py`
Child-2 fixture lives in `tests/unit/test_eval_solver_project_criterion.py` (confirmed by reading that file)
Plan uses 'extend shared file' implying the file pre-exists — it does not
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` does not exist — the plan describes it as a pre-existing 'shared file' to be extended, but it is absent from the test suite; the actual child-2 file is `test_eval_solver_project_criterion.py`.
findings.location: Scope section — `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py — extend shared file (reuses child 2's synthetic-repo fixture, adding a .eval.md spec for project.foo)`
scenarios: Implementors may search for a file to extend and find nothing, causing confusion about where the new tests should live.
The `-k eval` selector in the AC may match unintended tests if the file name is wrong.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'extend shared file' to 'new file'; add a note that the `_make_repo` / `FakeRunner` fixture pattern from `test_eval_solver_project_criterion.py` should be adapted (not imported) for the code-review variant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` overload (cross-ref `fc72-bf7e-7652-4228`) is a prerequisite deliverable — either confirm it is already provided by a named foundation child or add it to this plan's scope with its mapping contract (`project.foo` → `code-review-project-foo`).
evidence: Plan text: 'resolves the physical prompt id via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]'
Existing function signature at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36`: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str:` — no `gate_key` parameter
The cross-reference token `fc72-bf7e-7652-4228` has no match in the codebase (search returned no hits), suggesting it belongs to a not-yet-merged foundation child
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` referenced in the plan's `calibrate_criterion` code-review branch does not exist: the function at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36` accepts only one argument (`criterion_id: str`) with no `gate_key` parameter. If this signature is not introduced by a foundation/sibling child before this plan is implemented, `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch has no mechanism to map the logical id `project.foo` to the physical prompt id `code-review-project-foo`, breaking the data flow at the spec-load site.
scenarios: Implementer calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` and gets a `TypeError` at runtime because the function doesn't accept `gate_key`.

If the implementer silently falls back to the existing function, `project.foo` → `plan-review-project-foo` rather than `code-review-project-foo`, causing `load_eval_spec` to load the wrong (or nonexistent) spec.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'code-review-'` constant and a gate-aware overload (or second function `code_review_criterion_prompt_id`) in `criteria/ids.py` that maps `project.<name>` → `code-review-project-<name>`. Reference it explicitly in the plan so the implementer knows exactly what to call.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either extend `criterion_prompt_id` with a `gate_key` parameter (and a `"code-review-"` prefix branch) or introduce a separate `code_review_criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id) -> str` helper that maps `project.foo` → `code-review-project-foo`; update the plan's Approach and Scope sections to name the new/changed symbol.
evidence: Plan asserts: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review") [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]`
Actual function signature at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36`: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str:` — one positional parameter, no `gate_key`
Function body always prefixes with `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "plan-review-"`, never `"code-review-"`
`_code_review_prompt_id` at `eval_solver.py:194` guards with `if not prompt_id.startswith("code-review-"): return None`, so `"plan-review-project-foo"` would be rejected
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` does not accept a `gate_key` parameter; calling it as `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` will raise `TypeError` at runtime, and even if it did accept the argument, its hardcoded `"plan-review-"` prefix would produce the wrong physical id for the code-review gate (needed: `"code-review-project-<name>"`, produced: `"plan-review-project-<name>"`).
scenarios: Implementing the code-review branch in `calibrate_criterion` exactly as written will raise `TypeError: criterion_prompt_id() got an unexpected keyword argument 'gate_key'`

Even if the signature is fixed by adding `gate_key`, the id produced (`plan-review-project-foo`) does not pass `_code_review_prompt_id`'s prefix guard, silently falling through to `None` and failing to route to the code-review arm
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "code-review-"` to `ids.py` and a new helper `code_review_criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str` that returns `f"{CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"`. Reference this helper in the `calibrate_criterion` code-review branch and in `_code_review_prompt_id`'s admission check (alongside `effective_criteria`) so the full chain `project.foo` → `code-review-project-foo` → admitted by prefix guard → `_run_code_review_case` is consistent.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
